def solve(nums : Array(Int64)) : Int64
  # Precompute sieve and SPF
  max_num = nums.max || 1
  # But to handle 1e6, set max_num to 1e6
  max_num = 1_000_000

  # Precompute sieve
  sieve = Array.new(max_num + 1) { true }
  sieve[0] = false
  sieve[1] = false
  (2..Math.sqrt(max_num)).each do |i|
    if sieve[i]
      (i*i..max_num).step(i) do |j|
        sieve[j] = false
      end
    end
  end

  # Precompute SPF
  spf = Array.new(max_num + 1) { 0 }
  (2..max_num).each do |i|
    if spf[i] == 0
      spf[i] = i
      (i*2..max_num).step(i) do |j|
        if spf[j] == 0
          spf[j] = i
        end
      end
    end
  end

  # Function to factorize a number into its unique prime factors
  def factorize(x)
    return [] if x == 1
    factors = Set.new
    while x > 1
      p = spf[x]
      factors.add(p)
      while x % p == 0
        x /= p
      end
    end
    factors.to_a
  end

  # Build the map from primes to indices
  prime_to_indices = Hash.new { |h, k| h[k] = [] }
  nums.each_with_index do |num, index|
    if num == 1
      next
    end
    factors = factorize(num)
    factors.each do |p|
      prime_to_indices[p] << index
    end
  end

  # BFS
  n = nums.size
  if n == 0
    return 0
  end
  visited = Array.new(n) { -1 }
  queue = Queue.new
  queue << [0, 0]
  visited[0] = 0

  while !queue.empty?
    current, steps = queue.pop
    if current == n - 1
      return steps
    end
    # Check adjacent indices
    for next_index in [current - 1, current + 1]
      if next_index >= 0 && next_index < n && visited[next_index] == -1
        visited[next_index] = steps + 1
        queue << [next_index, steps + 1]
      end
    end
    # Check if current number is a prime
    if sieve[nums[current]]
      prime = nums[current]
      indices = prime_to_indices[prime]
      indices.each do |j|
        if j != current && visited[j] == -1
          visited[j] = steps + 1
          queue << [j, steps + 1]
        end
      end
    end
  end

  # If not found, return -1 (but according to problem statement, it's always possible)
  -1
end
